sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the layout clearly:
- Block is at rows 30-34, cols 19-23
- Charcoal corridor: rows 30-39, cols 29-33 (5 wide), then turns at rows 40-44, cols 25-34 (10 wide)
- The corridor is 5 columns wide (cols 29-33) in the vertical section
The block is 4 columns wide. The corridor is 5 columns wide. So the block can fit through the corridor!
Looking at the ascii:
```
30: OOGGGGGcccccGG  <- block (OO) at cols 19-22, gap (GGGGG) at cols 23-27, corridor (ccccc) at cols 28-32
```
Wait, let me re-read. The ascii shows:
- Row 30: `OOGGGGGcccccGG` starting from col 22
- So cols 22-23 are 'OO' (orange), cols 24-28 are 'GGGGG' (gray), cols 29-33 are 'ccccc' (charcoal)
But the block boundary says orange is at [[30, 19], [30, 23], [31, 23], [31, 19]], which means cols 19-22 (4 wide). And the ascii crop starts at col 22, so I see cols 22-23 as 'OO'.
So the block is at cols 19-22, and the corridor starts at col 28-29. There's a gap of gray cells between them.
To move the block into the corridor, I need to move RIGHT several times. The block needs to get to cols 28-31 (or 29-32) to be in the corridor.
Let me try moving RIGHT multiple times to get the block into the corridor, then DOWN.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Block at rows 30-34, cols 19-22 (orange) / 19-22 (blue)
# Corridor at cols 28-32 (5 wide)
# Need to move RIGHT to get block into corridor
# Block is 4 wide, corridor is 5 wide - should fit
# Move RIGHT 6 times to align block with corridor (cols 25-28 -> need to reach cols 28-31)
# Actually let me calculate: block cols 19-22, corridor cols 28-32
# Need block to be at cols 28-31 to fit in corridor
# That's 28 - 19 = 9 steps RIGHT... but there might be obstacles
# Let me try RIGHT first and see
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
